calendar.matters:
COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Jan-26-2024 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, being set for hearing on Plaintiff's request for entry of default judgment.

Dept. 505; Judge Maria Evangelista; Clerk N Webb; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 9:20 am and concluding at 9:30 am.

Plaintiff Pavel Flider is represented by Kenneth Brunetti, Esq.
Defendant Kirill Deninzon not appearing.
Defendant Natasha Nesterova-Denninzon not appearing.

Plaintiffs counsel presents summary of facts and addresses the Court in regards to Costs and Intrest.

The Court takes notice of Plaintiffs Declaration of Kenneth Brunetti in support of Costs and Intrest.

The matter is submitted.
By the statements made by Plaintiff and its counsel, documents filed, and evidence presented, the Court finds that Plaintiff has provided sufficient evidence proving each element of the complaint.

Good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff Pavel Flider and against Defendants Kirill Deninzon and Natasha Nesterova-Denninzon as follows:

1. Damages: $150,100.00
2. Prejudgment Interest: $220,303.90
3. Costs: $2,585.21

TOTAL JUDGMENT: $372,889.11
Proposed judgment signed and filed.(D505)

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Jan-05-2024 Continued To Jan-26-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 505

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Dec-18-2023 Assigned To Trial Court

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Dec-18-2023 Continued To Jan-05-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 505
MINI MINUTES: This matter is on calendar sent forthwith from Department 206 for Default Judgment Prove-Up Hearing as to Defendant Kirill Deninzon and Defendant Natasha Nesterova-Deninzon.

Dept. 505; Judge: Maria Evangelista; Clerk: K. Valdes; Bailiff: Deputy Prado; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 10:20:27 a.m., pausing at 10:36:17 a.m., resuming at 11:00:11 a.m. and concluding at 11:08:58 a.m.(D505)

Ken Brunetti, Esq., representing Plaintiff Pavel Flider who is present, was placed under oath.
Defendant Kirill Deninzon and Defendant Natasha Nesterova-Deninzon not appearing.

Plaintiff's counsel to summarize the facts of the case, causes of action and to provide offer of proof as to what the witness will attest to.

The Court takes judicial notice of the Complaint filed with the court on September 13, 2021.

Plaintiff requests the Court to include sanctions previously ordered by a different department as part of the judgment.

A brief recess was taken by the court to allow for counsel to compute for interest.

Session reconvenes and the Court continues the matter to January 5, 2024 at 9:00 a.m. in
Dept. 505 for Plaintiff to submit exhibits, declaration on the interest and costs. Counsel to lodge a proposed judgment.

The Court to call this matter first on the next hearing day.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Facts Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-20-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom Conference
MATTHEW S. SEPUYA, Esq., BRUNETTI ROUGEAU LLP, 415-992-8957, msepuya@brlawsf.com, for Plaintiff Pavel Flider.
KIRILL DENINZON, 415-994-8254, Defendant in Pro Per

All parties stipulate to Judge Pro Tem William Lynn hearing today's motions. All Parties and Judge Pro Tem William Lynn authorize the clerk to sign the stipulation on their behalf.

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF PAVEL FLIDER'S Motion To Deem Facts Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions. Motion as to Defendant Natasha Nesterova-Deninzon is denied. Motion as to Defendant Krill Deninzon is denied as moot as Defendant has served substantially-compliant responses. Request for sanctions is granted as to Defendant Krill Deninzon in the amount of $1,500.00 payable within 30 days of service of Notice of Entry of Order. The moving party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC3.1312 and must e-mail it to Judge Pro Tem William Lynn at williamclynn@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: William Lynn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
